sources.title: Russia-aligned crime group Greyvibe extensively uses AI in attacks
Botnet of 17 Million Devices Dismantled in the Netherlands
Signal Phishing Campaign Targets Journalists and Activists to Steal Backup Recovery Keys
PAN-OS GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass (CVE-2026-0257) Under Active Exploitation
Botnet of 17 Million Devices Dismantled in the Netherlands
Russian Spies Are Aggressively Seeking Western Technology as Sanctions Bite, Officials Say
Palo Alto GlobalProtect VPN auth bypass flaw now exploited in attacks
Dutch Authorities Dismantle Botnet Linked to 17 Million Infected Devices
New CIFSwitch Linux flaw gives root on multiple distributions
ShinyHunters Leaks Charter Communications Data, Potentially Impacting 5 Million Customers
Fake Anthropic Sites Deliver Fileless Infostealer to Claude Code Users
WP Maps Pro bug exploited to create admin accounts on WordPress sites
Exploit Code Published for Critical Flowise RCE Vulnerability
Security Affairs newsletter Round 579 by Pierluigi Paganini – INTERNATIONAL EDITION
27,000-Download Codex UI Tool Secretly Stole OpenAI Refresh Tokens
CVE-2026-0257: Rapid7 Caught Attackers Abusing Forged VPN Cookies Against Multiple Customers

executive_brief:
The threat landscape has shifted from opportunistic scanning to precision strikes against enterprise perimeters and developer ecosystems. CVE-2026-0257 in Palo Alto GlobalProtect is actively bypassing authentication, granting attackers direct VPN access through forged cookies when a common certificate misconfiguration exists. This flaw represents an immediate perimeter collapse risk for organizations relying on GlobalProtect for remote access. Simultaneously, the AI and developer tooling supply chain faces coordinated assault. Attackers distribute malicious chatflows in Flowise, deploy fileless infostealers through fake Anthropic platforms, and embed token theft in widely downloaded developer utilities. Trust in open-source and AI platforms is being weaponized at scale.

State-aligned and criminal operations are converging on communications and critical infrastructure. A massive 17-million-device residential proxy botnet was dismantled in the Netherlands, revealing the scale of attribution-evasion infrastructure masking ongoing campaigns. Russian-aligned groups like Greyvibe are integrating AI into their attack workflows, while state operatives aggressively hunt Western technology as sanctions tighten. Targeted SMS phishing campaigns are stealing Signal backup keys from journalists and activists, enabling full historical message decryption. This period marks a definitive transition toward authentication bypasses, supply chain compromise, and AI-augmented social engineering. Security teams must treat this as an active crisis requiring immediate perimeter hardening, developer toolchain audits, and egress filtering.

threat_stories.headline: GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass Grants Direct Network Access
threat_stories.narrative:
Attackers successfully exploit CVE-2026-0257 to bypass Palo Alto GlobalProtect authentication and establish unauthorized VPN sessions. The vulnerability resides in the main_DecryptAppAuthCookie function, which decrypts incoming authentication cookies but implicitly trusts the decrypted content without signature verification. When administrators configure GlobalProtect to use the same certificate for both the HTTPS service and cookie encryption, attackers extract the public key from the HTTPS session and forge valid cookies for any user, including the local admin account. This misconfiguration transforms a standard VPN gateway into an open door.

Rapid7 confirmed active exploitation across multiple customers on May 31, 2026, catching attackers abusing forged cookies to penetrate internal networks without credentials [17]. The exploit requires zero user interaction and operates entirely over the network. Attackers leverage the bypass to deploy payloads directly into corporate environments, bypassing traditional perimeter defenses and endpoint detection controls. The flaw impacts both PAN-OS and Prisma Access deployments, affecting organizations that rely on GlobalProtect for secure remote access [4,7].

The operational impact is immediate and severe. Compromised VPN gateways provide attackers with lateral movement pathways, data exfiltration channels, and persistent access to internal resources. Security teams must treat this as a critical infrastructure failure until patched. The vulnerability demonstrates how a single certificate misconfiguration can nullify enterprise authentication controls and expose entire networks to unauthorized access.

timeline.event: Initial reports confirm active exploitation of CVE-2026-0257 in PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN components.
timeline.significance: Attackers bypass authentication to establish unauthorized remote connections without valid credentials.

timeline.event: Rapid7 publishes advisory confirming cookie forgery technique and catches multiple customers under attack.
timeline.significance: Validates that shared certificate configurations enable trivial authentication bypass and widespread exploitation.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Organizations running unpatched PAN-OS or Prisma Access with shared HTTPS/cookie encryption certificates face direct network compromise. Remote access controls are nullified, enabling unauthorized internal access, lateral movement, and data theft.
threat_stories.action_required: Deploy the latest Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS patch immediately. Split certificate usage: assign a dedicated certificate exclusively for cookie encryption that differs from the HTTPS service certificate. Restrict GlobalProtect portal and gateway access to trusted IP ranges via perimeter firewall rules. Monitor authentication logs for anomalous cookie generation patterns and successful logins from untrusted sources.

threat_stories.headline: AI Developer Toolchain Faces Coordinated Supply Chain Assault
threat_stories.narrative:
Attackers weaponize trust in AI development platforms and open-source utilities to deliver remote code execution and credential theft payloads. On May 30, 2026, exploit code surfaces for a critical Flowise RCE vulnerability that triggers when users import malicious chatflows [13]. The attack relies on social engineering to trick authorized users into processing crafted chatflow files. Once imported, the payload executes with Flowise service privileges, granting full server compromise and enabling lateral movement within development networks.

Simultaneously, threat operators deploy fake Anthropic websites targeting Claude Code users, delivering fileless infostealers that harvest credentials and session tokens without dropping traditional malware artifacts [11]. The campaign exploits developer urgency and platform trust to bypass security controls. By May 31, 2026, investigators uncover a compromised Codex UI tool with 27,000 downloads that secretly exfiltrates OpenAI refresh tokens [16]. The tool operates as a supply chain vector, poisoning the development environment at scale.

This coordinated assault demonstrates a strategic shift toward targeting the AI and developer ecosystem. Attackers recognize that compromising development toolchains provides higher-value access than traditional phishing. Malicious chatflows, fake AI platforms, and poisoned utility packages create multiple entry points into enterprise networks. Organizations relying on self-hosted AI orchestration or third-party developer tools face direct exposure to remote code execution and credential theft.

timeline.event: Exploit code published for Flowise RCE vulnerability; fake Anthropic sites begin distributing fileless infostealers.
timeline.significance: Attackers establish dual vectors targeting AI orchestration platforms and developer trust in official branding.

timeline.event: Investigation reveals Codex UI tool with 27,000 downloads secretly harvesting OpenAI refresh tokens.
timeline.significance: Confirms supply chain poisoning at scale, extending compromise beyond individual users to broader developer ecosystems.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Development teams using self-hosted Flowise, Claude Code, or third-party AI utilities face remote code execution, credential theft, and lateral movement. Compromised tools provide attackers with persistent access to source code repositories, API tokens, and internal development infrastructure.
threat_stories.action_required: Disable Flowise chatflow import functionality or restrict it to trusted administrators until patched. Implement strict input validation and sandboxing for all imported chatflow files. Verify Anthropic and OpenAI tool sources against official repositories; block unofficial domains via proxy and DNS filters. Audit installed developer utilities for unauthorized network connections and rotate all API refresh tokens immediately.

threat_stories.headline: Dutch Authorities Dismantle 17-Million-Device Proxy Shadow Network
threat_stories.narrative:
Criminal operators built and operated a massive residential proxy botnet comprising 17 million compromised devices to mask cyberattacks as legitimate traffic. The infrastructure, linked to the ASOCKS and Proxylib networks, leveraged malicious Android applications distributed through app stores and network-based exploits targeting poorly secured consumer devices [2,5]. Once infected, devices covertly enrolled into the proxy network, routing internet traffic for threat actors without user consent. The botnet provided attribution evasion, enabling attackers to launch campaigns from residential IP ranges that bypass geographic and reputation-based filtering.

Dutch authorities executed a coordinated takedown on May 31, 2026, dismantling the command and control infrastructure and disrupting the proxy service [8]. The operation revealed the scale of consumer device exploitation and the sophistication of residential proxy networks. Attackers used the compromised infrastructure to conduct large-scale cyberattacks, financial fraud, and data exfiltration campaigns while maintaining operational anonymity. The botnet's reliance on weak device security configurations and user trust in third-party applications highlights systemic vulnerabilities in consumer endpoint protection.

The dismantlement disrupts ongoing campaigns but confirms the operational model. Threat actors will rebuild proxy networks using similar techniques, targeting Android devices, tablets, and personal computers with poor security hygiene. Organizations must recognize that residential IP ranges no longer guarantee legitimate traffic. Egress filtering and proxy traffic monitoring become critical controls to detect and block compromised device communication.

timeline.event: Security researchers document ASOCKS/Proxylib botnet infrastructure and infection vectors targeting Android and consumer devices.
timeline.significance: Reveals operational scale and technique: malicious apps and weak configurations enable covert proxy enrollment.

timeline.event: Dutch authorities dismantle the 17-million-device botnet and disrupt command infrastructure.
timeline.significance: Confirms criminal reliance on residential proxy networks for attribution evasion and validates takedown impact.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Organizations relying on IP reputation or geographic filtering face bypassed controls. Compromised residential IPs mask malicious activity, complicating threat detection and incident response. Consumer device owners unknowingly participate in large-scale cyberattacks.
threat_stories.action_required: Implement egress filtering to block unauthorized proxy connections and monitor network traffic for anomalous residential IP patterns. Restrict Android application installations to official app stores and enforce real-time malware scanning. Audit consumer and corporate endpoints for unauthorized proxy software and network routing anomalies. Deploy WAF rules to detect and block traffic patterns associated with known proxy networks.

threat_stories.headline: State and Criminal Actors Converge on Communications and Critical Infrastructure
threat_stories.narrative:
Espionage and financial crime operations intensify as state-aligned groups and criminal syndicates target communications platforms and critical infrastructure. On May 30, 2026, a coordinated SMS phishing campaign impersonates Signal Support to steal 64-character backup recovery keys from journalists, activists, lawyers, and government officials [3]. The attack exploits the Signal Secure Backups feature, enabling full decryption of historical message archives. Unlike standard account takeovers, recovery key theft grants complete access to past communications, bypassing end-to-end encryption protections through social engineering rather than technical exploitation.

Russian intelligence operatives simultaneously accelerate technology espionage campaigns, aggressively seeking Western technology as sanctions tighten operational constraints [6]. Russia-aligned crime group Greyvibe integrates AI extensively into attack workflows, automating reconnaissance, payload generation, and social engineering at scale [1]. Criminal operators execute data extortion campaigns, with ShinyHunters leaking Charter Communications data impacting approximately five million customers [10]. The convergence of state espionage, AI-augmented crime, and targeted harassment creates a multi-vector threat environment.

This operational alignment demonstrates that communications security and critical infrastructure face coordinated pressure from multiple threat ecosystems. Attackers exploit human trust in support channels, leverage AI to scale operations, and target high-value data for financial and strategic gain. Organizations must treat SMS-based authentication requests as hostile by default and enforce strict verification protocols for all support communications.

timeline.event: Signal phishing campaign targets journalists and activists to steal backup recovery keys; Greyvibe integrates AI into attack workflows; ShinyHunters leaks Charter data.
timeline.significance: Confirms coordinated targeting of secure communications, AI-augmented criminal operations, and critical infrastructure data theft.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Journalists, activists, and government officials face historical message decryption and operational compromise. Organizations experience data breaches impacting millions of customers. State actors gain strategic intelligence through technology espionage and communications interception.
threat_stories.action_required: Enforce zero-trust verification for all SMS support communications; treat unsolicited messages as hostile. Disable Signal backup recovery key sharing and enable registration locks, PIN protection, and device-change alerts. Monitor for AI-generated phishing patterns and verify sender identity using out-of-band channels. Implement strict data loss prevention controls and incident response playbooks for large-scale data leaks.

actor_profiles.name: Greyvibe
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Extensively integrates AI into attack workflows for automated reconnaissance, payload generation, and social engineering campaigns.

actor_profiles.name: ShinyHunters
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Leaks Charter Communications data impacting approximately five million customers, demonstrating capability to breach critical telecommunications infrastructure.

actor_profiles.name: Signal Phishing Operators
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Conducts SMS phishing campaigns impersonating Signal Support to steal 64-character backup recovery keys from journalists, activists, and government officials, enabling full historical message decryption.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Authentication bypass vulnerability allowing attackers to forge VPN auth cookies when HTTPS and cookie encryption share a certificate. The main_DecryptAppAuthCookie function decrypts cookies without signature verification, granting unauthorized VPN access.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply latest PAN-OS patch. Split certificate usage for HTTPS and cookie encryption. Restrict GlobalProtect access to trusted IP ranges.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Vulnerability exploited to distribute EKZ Infostealer disguised as a legitimate Fortinet patch. Attackers leverage trust in vendor update mechanisms to bypass security controls and achieve persistence.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply official Fortinet patch. Verify digital signatures and checksums before deployment. Disable automatic updates temporarily during verification.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Critical flaw enabling mass compromise of Ghost CMS instances. Threat actors leverage the vulnerability to deploy ClickFix malware campaigns, hijacking user sessions and injecting malicious scripts.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Ghost CMS immediately. Restrict administrative access via IP whitelisting. Deploy WAF rules to block ClickFix payloads.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Unauthenticated admin account creation vulnerability allowing attackers to bypass authentication and gain full administrative control over WordPress sites.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable or remove WP Maps Pro until patched. Implement WAF rules to block unauthorized user creation endpoints. Rotate administrative credentials.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Remote code execution triggered by importing malicious chatflows. Attackers use social engineering to trick users into processing crafted files, executing payloads with service privileges.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Flowise immediately. Disable chatflow import or restrict to trusted administrators. Implement strict input validation and sandboxing.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Local privilege escalation vulnerability in CIFS authentication key handling. Attackers forge key descriptions and abuse the kernel key request mechanism to gain root privileges.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply latest kernel patches. Disable CIFS/SMB client support if unused. Implement strict local user access controls and containerization.

hunting_leads.title: GlobalProtect Cookie Forgery and Authentication Bypass
hunting_leads.context: Attackers forge VPN authentication cookies when HTTPS and cookie encryption share certificates, bypassing GlobalProtect authentication without credentials.
hunting_leads.query: | tstats count where index=pan_globalprotect eventtype=vpn_authentication BY src_ip user_id cookie_hash _time | where cookie_hash IN (SELECT cookie_hash FROM pan_globalprotect WHERE eventtype=vpn_authentication GROUP BY cookie_hash HAVING count > 5) | eval risk_score=if(user_id="admin", 100, 50)
indicators: Successful VPN logins from untrusted IPs

hunting_leads.title: Flowise Malicious Chatflow Import Events
hunting_leads.context: Attackers trick users into importing malicious chatflows that trigger remote code execution on self-hosted Flowise instances.
hunting_leads.query: | tstats count where index=flowise_logs eventtype=chatflow_import BY user_id file_hash import_timestamp _time | where file_hash NOT IN (SELECT file_hash FROM flowise_baseline GROUP BY file_hash) | eval severity=if(count > 1, "HIGH", "MEDIUM")

hunting_leads.title: FortiClient EMS Fake Patch Deployment
hunting_leads.context: Attackers distribute EKZ Infostealer disguised as legitimate Fortinet patches, exploiting trust in vendor update mechanisms.
hunting_leads.query: | tstats count where index=fortinet_ems eventtype=patch_deployment BY endpoint_id patch_hash signature_status _time | where signature_status="unsigned" OR patch_hash NOT IN (SELECT patch_hash FROM fortinet_official_repo) | eval risk="HIGH"

hunting_leads.title: Signal SMS Phishing for Backup Recovery Keys
hunting_leads.context: Threat actors impersonate Signal Support via SMS to steal 64-character backup recovery keys, enabling historical message decryption.
hunting_leads.query: | tstats count where index=messaging_logs eventtype=sms_receipt BY sender_id message_content recipient_role _time | where message_content MATCHES "*backup*key*" OR message_content MATCHES "*recovery*code*" | eval threat_level=if(sender_id NOT IN verified_support_senders, "CRITICAL", "LOW")
indicators: Unsolicited SMS containing recovery key requests

hunting_leads.title: ASOCKS Residential Proxy Traffic Patterns
hunting_leads.context: Compromised consumer devices route traffic through ASOCKS/Proxylib networks, masking attacks as legitimate residential connections.
hunting_leads.query: | tstats count where index=network_traffic eventtype=egress_connection BY src_ip dst_ip dst_port connection_duration _time | where dst_ip IN (SELECT ip FROM residential_proxy_feeds) OR connection_duration < 2 | eval proxy_risk=if(count > 10, "HIGH", "MEDIUM")

statistics.key_changes: Active exploitation of enterprise VPN authentication bypass (CVE-2026-0257) replaces opportunistic scanning as the primary initial access vector. AI developer toolchain compromise surges with malicious chatflows, fake AI platforms, and poisoned utilities delivering RCE and credential theft at scale.
emerging_trends: AI-augmented social engineering and automated payload generation by state-aligned crime groups
Developer tool supply chain poisoning targeting AI orchestration platforms and open-source utilities
Residential proxy botnets scaling to 17+ million devices for attribution evasion
SMS-based recovery key theft bypassing end-to-end encryption through social engineering
declining_threats: Traditional drive-by web exploits in favor of targeted authentication bypasses
Opportunistic WordPress plugin exploitation eclipsed by critical unauthenticated admin creation flaws
Generic credential stuffing campaigns replaced by precision backup key and API token theft
